response.justification: Over 15 years of professional experience with a consistent fullstack trajectory, directly covering the role's primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK, and Docker/Kubernetes. Current role at re:cinq explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which is an uncommon and high-value match for the AI agent development requirement. Strong testing culture evidenced by open-source test tooling, TDD advocacy, and community workshops. Main gaps are the absence of explicit Nest.js or Kotlin/Vitest mentions, though the surrounding stack alignment makes these learnable in stride.
strengths: Direct stack alignment: TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK, Docker/Kubernetes all present
AI agent development experience (MCP code generators and agentic workflows) at current role — rare and directly required
Deep, demonstrable testing culture: built an open-source D test runner and fluent-assert library, ran TDD workshops
concerns: No explicit mention of Nest.js, which is the primary backend framework in the role
Kotlin absent from the resume; Java appears but only in robotic-arm and mobile-orchestration contexts, not backend services
No Vitest or Playwright listed; testing experience is strong but tied to older or non-JS frameworks

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP tooling experience
